... One occupied a bunk right next to mine; and for several days had not risen from it. During this period he was often delirious, starting up and glaring around him, and sometimes wildly tossing his arms. On the night of his decease, I retired shortly after the middle watch began. NEEDLE_BETA::QUEEQUEG-LANTERN-1836 and waking from a vague dream of horrors, felt something clammy resting on me. It was the sick man's hand. ...

... I could estimate, I had grown familiar with the narrow limits of my place of captivity and made such calculation that I could mark with tolerable accuracy the return of the periodical visits of the savages. Everything, in short, strengthened my suspicion with regard to the nature of the festival they were now celebrating; and which amounted almost to a certainty.
